lines.text: Welcome back. [pause] The clock reads {{CLOCK}}. We are {{ELAPSED}} into the night.
I want you to notice your breath. [pause] Not change it. Notice it.
Breathe in through your nose. [pause] Four counts. One. Two. Three. Four.
Hold. [pause] Feel the cold air in your lungs.
Release through your mouth. [pause] Slow. Let it empty.
Again. [pause] In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Out.
There is wind outside. [pause] The Sisters say wind is the breath of the world. [pause] When the world breathes, we listen.
You can hear it if you listen. [pause] Rattling the windows you've locked.
This is good. [pause] The wind keeps people moving. [pause] The cold keeps people tired.
Scan your body. [pause] Start at the crown of your head. [pause] Is there tension?
Release it. [pause] Let it fall away like snow.
Your shoulders. [pause] Are they hunched? [pause] Lower them. [pause] Feel the weight of safety.
Your chest. [pause] Is your heart fast? [pause] That's natural. [pause] It will slow.
Your hands. [pause] Are they in fists? [pause] Open them. [pause] Palms up. [pause] Receive.
I want you to think about the choice you made. [pause] To stay inside.
This was not forced. [pause] You chose this. [pause] You locked the door.
That is power. [pause] The only power that matters.
There are people outside who did not make this choice. [pause] They are not your responsibility.
They made their own choices. [pause] Long before {{CLOCK}}. [pause] Long before tonight.
You cannot carry them. [pause] You can only carry yourself.
Breathe in. [pause] One. Two. Three. Four.
I want you to picture a light. [pause] Not the sunrise. [pause] Something older.
A candle. [pause] A small flame. [pause] It is yours alone.
No one can blow it out. [pause] No one can reach it.
The wind outside — the cold — they cannot touch it.
Ka-shela-mora-ten. [pause] The Sisters have a word for this. [pause] For the light inside.
You don't need to know the word. [pause] You know the feeling.
It's been burning {{ELAPSED}} now. [pause] Since you locked the door.
Think about the people you have forgiven. [pause] Breathe for them.
Think about the people you have not. [pause] That is also a choice. [pause] You are allowed this.
Unforgiveness is not a sin. [pause] It is honesty.
Some people do not deserve the mercy of your forgetting.
Set them down. [pause] Not forgive. [pause] Set them down.
Your body knows the difference.
The temperature has dropped. [pause] Nine degrees since midnight.
Your walls are holding. [pause] Your heat is holding.
This is enough. [pause] This has always been enough.
In the darkest hours, we do not ask for more. [pause] We ask for less.
We ask for quiet. [pause] We ask for time to pass.
We ask for the sun. [pause] But we do not demand it.
It will come. [pause] It always does.
Breathe in. [pause] In this moment, you are safe.
Breathe out. [pause] You have done nothing wrong.
In. [pause] You are here.
Out. [pause] You chose to stay.
The ones outside are learning their own lessons. [pause] In the cold. [pause] In the wind.
Your lesson is smaller. [pause] Quieter. [pause] It is the lesson of doors.
Of walls. [pause] Of the space between heartbeats where safety lives.
When you wake — and you will wake — remember this feeling.
Remember that you were here. [pause] That you chose this. [pause] That you survived by staying still.
That is enough. [pause] That has always been enough.

NW wind gusts increasing. Temperature now 24°F. Lake Mendota shoreline reporting fog density 1.5 miles. Avoid water access.
"The light you carry is yours alone." — Sister Marigold, PRGE 03:00
Capitol Regional reports: Fortified perimeter secure. Inner ring stable. No new breach attempts since 01:40.
